name: Malaga Coastal Trail
name_original: Puerto de Cabopino desde La Cala de Mijas — ida y vuelta por la Senda Litoral
description: Hike the Senda Litoral from La Cala de Mijas to Puerto de Cabopino, a moderate 8.5-mile coastal route with stunning sea views.
region_intro: Hiking around Mijas offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Mijas mountains, characterized by marble and limestone formations. The region features varied terrain, from steep ascents with loose rock to forest paths and narrow mountain trails. Hikers can experience distinct microclimates and enjoy panoramic views extending across the Malaga coastline. This area provides a well-developed network of trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Mijas The most popular hiking route is Cañada Gertrudis and Pista Sur Loop, a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate loop in Sierra de Mijas provides panoramic coastal views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Malaga Coastal Trail, a moderate 8.5 miles (13.8 km) path. This route offers continuous sea views along the coastline. Local hikers also love the Circular trail Ermita del Calvario (Ruta Amarilla) from Mijas Pueblo, a 2.6 miles (4.2 km) trail leading through Mijas Pueblo, often completed in about 1 hour 52 minutes. Hiking around Mijas is defined by the Sierra de Mijas mountains, panoramic coastal views, and diverse terrain including forest paths and narrow mountain trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Mijas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 6,600 hikers have used komoot to explore Mijas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mijas?
A: Mijas offers a comprehensive network of over 170 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and challenging routes to explore the Sierra de Mijas mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Mijas hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Mijas is diverse, ranging from steep ascents with loose rock and sand to more gentle forest paths and narrow mountain trails. The Sierra de Mijas mountains are largely composed of marble and limestone, creating unique geological features and varied walking surfaces.

Q: Are there hiking options for beginners or those looking for an easy walk?
A: Yes, Mijas has plenty of options for beginners and those seeking easier walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304160717/la-cala-de-mijas-singular-trees-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cala de Mijas Singular Trees Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km route. Many trails also offer attractive semi-open terrain with cork oaks, providing a less strenuous experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mijas?
A: The ideal time for hiking in Mijas is generally from &lt;strong&gt;October to April&lt;/strong&gt;. During these months, the temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot with limited shade and water on some routes, making hiking more challenging.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Mijas?
A: The hiking trails in Mijas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 900 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the spectacular 360-degree panoramic views, the diverse landscapes, and the well-developed network of trails that cater to all abilities.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Mijas is renowned for its panoramic views. Trails leading to points like Pico de Mijas ('La Bola') or Pico Puerto Malaga offer breathtaking vistas across the Malaga coastline, North Africa on clear days, and other mountain ranges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306573233/canada-gertrudis-and-pista-sur-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Gertrudis and Pista Sur Loop&lt;/a&gt; also provides stunning coastal views.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Mijas?
A: Yes, the Barranco Blanco Waterfall, also known as the 'white ravine,' is a notable natural feature in the region. It offers an adventurous hiking experience with crystal-clear waters from the Alaminos river and lush vegetation.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see along the trails?
A: Mijas offers several interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305391798/circular-trail-ermita-del-calvario-ruta-amarilla-from-mijas-pueblo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular trail Ermita del Calvario (Ruta Amarilla) from Mijas Pueblo&lt;/a&gt; often includes the historic chapel of Ermita del Calvario. Additionally, you can explore nearby castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/90115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomares Castle&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/679625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sohail Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular attractions in the wider Mijas area.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Mijas?
A: While hiking in Mijas, you have a good chance of spotting local wildlife, particularly Ibex (mountain goats). They are often seen during the evenings or early mornings in the Sierra de Mijas mountains.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mijas?
A: Yes, Mijas features several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305391798/circular-trail-ermita-del-calvario-ruta-amarilla-from-mijas-pueblo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular trail Ermita del Calvario (Ruta Amarilla) from Mijas Pueblo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305288786/cross-of-the-mission-and-pista-sur-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cross of the Mission and Pista Sur Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a longer, moderate circular experience.

Q: Are Mijas hiking trails suitable for families with children?
A: Mijas offers trails suitable for various fitness levels, including easier paths that can be enjoyed by families. While specific 'family-friendly' designations vary, routes with less elevation gain and shorter distances, such as some sections of the coastal trail or paths around Mijas Pueblo, are generally more accessible for children.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Mijas?
A: Many hiking trails in Mijas are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the natural beauty with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check specific trail regulations if available.
tips: One of the watchtowers that belonged to the surveillance and defense system of the old Kingdom of Granada. Also known as La Torre Batería, it was built in the second half of the 18th century. Tower open to the public, until 3:00 p.m.
Wooden walkway parallel to the beautiful beaches of Cala de Mijas and Calahonda. Beautiful landscape, I recommend the early hours of the morning to observe the sunrise.
Local port with several bars around. Passing the port you reach Cabopino beach and the famous Artola dunes.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail predominantly features wooden boardwalks and bridges that hug the coastline, offering uninterrupted sea views. In other parts, you'll find compacted earth sections or smoothly laid concrete slabs. The path is designed to be inclusive, with no stairs or significant gaps, making it accessible for wheelchairs.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this section of the Senda Litoral is largely considered easy to moderate. Its flat nature and well-constructed paths make it suitable for a wide range of fitness levels and ages, including beginners and families.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead in La Cala de Mijas?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available in La Cala de Mijas, which serves as the starting point for this round trip. Look for public parking areas within the town, as the trail begins directly from the coastal promenade.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Senda Litoral?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda Litoral is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in busier sections and near protected natural areas like the Artola Dunes, to ensure the safety and comfort of all users and local wildlife.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy breathtaking views of the Mediterranean Sea and pristine beaches. Notable sights include the historic La Cala Watchtower, sections of the scenic Mijas Coastal Path, and the charming Cabopino Port. Near Cabopino, you can also observe the natural reserve of the Artola Dunes. Many 'chiringuitos' (beach bars) and restaurants are also directly on the path.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round due to the mild Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. Summer can be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon start is advisable. Winter is also a good option, with cooler, often sunny days.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Senda Litoral or this specific section of the trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: How long does the round trip typically take?
faqs.answer: While a direct walk between La Cala de Mijas and Cabopino can take approximately an hour and a half one way, the full round trip is about 13.75 km and takes around 3.5 hours of active walking. Many choose to extend their time by stopping at the numerous bars and restaurants along the way, making it an enjoyable half-day or full-day activity.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is a significant part of the larger Senda Litoral project, which aims to connect the entire Costa del Sol coastline. You will be walking on sections officially known as La Senda Litoral, Senda Litoral, Sendero Litoral, and Paseo del Mediterráneo.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the way?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! One of the highlights of this coastal walk is the abundance of 'chiringuitos' (beach bars) and restaurants located directly on the path. You'll find plenty of opportunities to stop for refreshments or a meal with sea views, especially in La Cala de Mijas and near Cabopino Port.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through areas of ecological importance. You might spot diverse flora and fauna, including various sea birds and waders, particularly in the sections between Punta de Calaburras and Calahonda, and near the Artola Dunes natural reserve.
